steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: For a system of two linear equations to have infinitely many solutions, the equations must be identical — same slope and same y-intercept. The question asks only for the value of $b$ in $y = \frac{2}{5}x + b$. Matching this to $y = \frac{2}{5}x + 2$ requires $b = 2$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
Each choice gives a single value of $b$. The y-intercept of $y = \frac{2}{5}x + 2$ is $2$, so only $b = 2$ makes the equations identical.
  • Choice A — -2: wrong sign — $-2 \neq 2$. ✗
  • Choice B — $-\frac{1}{2}$: not the y-intercept of the given line. ✗
  • Choice C — $\frac{1}{2}$: not the y-intercept of the given line. ✗
  • Choice D — 2: matches the y-intercept exactly, making both equations identical. ✓
D is correct.
